title: Solar Particle Event
description: The orbital warning network detects an M-class solar flare. Lethal radiation will hit the rover in hours. We need to shield our living quarters immediately.

options.label: Deploy heavy lead-shielded blankets
options.log: We draped the heavy shielding but depleted some spare parts and rover batteries.

options.label: Bury the rover in regolith using the scoop
options.log: The scoop piled abrasive dust over the cabin. Hull suffered micro-scratches and battery drained.

options.label: Take anti-radiation meds and ride it out
options.log: We took chemical radioprotectants, but the crew suffered severe nausea and anxiety.

title: Electrostatic Dust Storm
description: A towering wall of red dust blocks the sun, generating high-voltage static charges that threaten our solar arrays and navigation systems.

options.label: Deploy grounding anchors and lock down
options.log: We anchored safely and waited out the storm, but consumed valuable rations and water.

options.label: Push through the storm using active radar
options.log: Static discharges scorched the outer hull sensors, rattling the crew's nerves.

title: Seized Drive Axle
description: A grinding shriek echoes from the rear chassis. Fine basaltic dust has bypassed the seals and locked the rear-left drive axle.

options.label: Perform an EVA to replace the drive gears
options.log: A grueling four-hour EVA in the cold replaced the gears but exhausted our oxygen and crew.

options.label: Weld the axle locked and drag the wheel
options.log: Dragging the dead wheel drains our batteries rapidly and stresses the chassis.

title: Abandoned Science Station
description: We spot the half-buried domes of an old, decommissioned automated science depot. Its airlock is breached, but supplies might remain inside.

options.label: Thoroughly scavenge the ruins
options.log: We recovered valuable spare components and oxygen, but the search drained power.

options.label: Recover only the heavy batteries
options.log: We hauled the heavy carbon batteries back, slightly denting our exterior airlock frame.

title: Airlock Gasket Leak
description: A high-pitched hiss warns of pressure loss. The primary airlock seal is degrading rapidly, venting atmosphere into the void.

options.label: Apply emergency liquid sealant
options.log: We patched the leak quickly, but lost some air and ruined a multi-purpose polymer sealant kit.

options.label: Let the airlock depressurize and bypass it
options.log: Venting the airlock saved the seal but cost a massive chunk of our breathable air supply.

title: Clogged CO2 Scrubber
description: Dust intrusion from the storm has bypassed the external intakes, clogging the primary amine CO2 scrubbers. Carbon dioxide levels are rising.

options.label: Flush the filters with recycled water
options.log: We flushed the amine beds. CO2 levels dropped, but we lost twenty liters of reusable water.

options.label: Swap in a replacement scrubber cartridge
options.log: We installed our last spare cartridge, immediately restoring cabin air quality.

title: Buried Cargo Lander
description: The radar pings a metallic anomaly beneath three meters of loose sand. It matches the signature of a lost uncrewed supply lander.

options.label: Excavate using the rover's heater-grid
options.log: Thermal melting of frozen crust consumed massive power, but revealed pristine rations and water.

options.label: Manually dig with hand tools
options.log: The crew dug for six exhausting hours. We found some parts, but morale plummeted from fatigue.

title: Cabin Window Micro-Fracture
description: A microscopic pebble kicked up by the storm has starred the thick polycarbonate viewport. Structural integrity is dropping.

options.label: Depressurize cabin and apply structural epoxy
options.log: Applying epoxy required venting the cabin. The patch holds, but we are cold and low on air.

options.label: Bolt an opaque aluminum cover over window
options.log: The window is secure, but the crew is now entombed in absolute darkness, spiking anxiety.

title: Cosmic Ray Bombardment
description: A spike in galactic cosmic rays is detected. The rover's active magnetic shield is struggling to deflect the high-energy iron nuclei.

options.label: Overclock the magnetic shield generator
options.log: We boosted the shields. It deflected the radiation but melted several secondary power conduits.

options.label: Retreat into the shielded sleeping bunks
options.log: The crew huddled in the cramped bunks for 36 hours. We used up rations while making zero progress.

title: Coolant Loop Rupture
description: A vital pipe in the radioisotope thermoelectric generator's coolant loop has burst, leaking hot glycol onto the main batteries.

options.label: Shut down the heater to patch the pipe
options.log: We patched the leak, but cabin temperatures plummeted, freezing some of our water.

options.label: Use the backup pump to isolate the leak
options.log: Isolating the leak saved our water, but the thermal spike damaged adjacent electrical systems.

title: Discarded Ascent Stage
description: We stumble upon the hollow shell of an old crewed ascent stage. The interior has been stripped, but the fuel cells remain.

options.label: Drain the fuel cell water-byproducts
options.log: We siphoned forty liters of stale, slightly metallic-tasting water from the ancient fuel cells.

options.label: Salvage the gold-foil insulation
options.log: We wrapped our vulnerable external cabling in salvaged thermal insulation, strengthening our hull.

title: Hydroponics Bay Leak
description: The sealing flange on the experimental micro-green incubator has failed. Dry Martian air is desorbing the moisture from our crops.

options.label: Flood the chamber with emergency nitrogen
options.log: Nitrogen purge saved the system, but the shock killed half of the edible green crops.

options.label: Redirect drinking water to maintain humidity
options.log: We sacrificed thirty liters of drinking water to save the harvest, gaining fresh rations.
